title: NOC 33102 – Nurse Aides, Orderlies, and Patient Service Associates | GTR Immigration
description: Nurse Aides, Orderlies, and Patient Service Associates under NOC 33102 are frontline healthcare support professionals providing direct patient care across Canada. With Canada’s aging population and expansion of long-term care services, this TEER 3 occupation represents one of the most in-demand healthcare support roles nationwide. For internationally trained candidates, NOC 33102 may qualify under Express Entry and multiple Provincial Nominee Programs, making it a strong pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER3 – Occupations usually require college training or on-the-job trainingMajor Group33 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesSub-Major Group331 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesMinor Group3310 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesNOC Code33102

duties: Nurse Aides and Orderlies perform some or all of the following duties:
Assist patients with bathing, dressing, grooming, and feeding
Monitor and record vital signs (blood pressure, temperature, pulse)
Document patient care and fluid intake/output
Provide emergency first aid within scope of training
Transport patients via wheelchair or stretcher
Assist with equipment setup and maintenance
employment_requirements: Completion of a Personal Support Worker (PSW), Nursing Aide, or Health Care Aide program
CPR and First Aid certification (usually required)
Appropriate driver’s license (for medical attendants or transfer roles)
International applicants may need credential assessment depending on provincial requirements.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $30,800 to $49,700, depending on province, facility type, and experience.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 33102 is very strong, supported by:
Growth in home and community care services
This occupation represents one of the largest projected healthcare employment growth areas in Canada.
labour_market_demand: Nurse Aides and related professionals are employed in:
This significant demand gap demonstrates strong employment prospects, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, Manitoba, and Saskatchewan.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Nurse Aide?
TEER 3 eligibility under Express Entry (program dependent)
Career progression into nursing or specialized healthcare roles
As healthcare systems expand and long-term care needs increase, Nurse Aides remain essential to patient care delivery.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 33102, you may be eligible for immigration pathways to Canada through Express Entry or Provincial Nominee Programs.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 33102.
